purpose: Decision-critical reconciliation of entity ownership, lease cash, residual acreage, water, railway, and related-party claims.

primary_sources.role: FY2025 audited income-tax-basis financial statements and notes

resolved_facts: The Corporation and related entities collectively report approximately 239,000 surface acres and 318,000 mineral acres; the word collectively prevents assigning all gross acreage automatically to AZLCZ shareholders.

FY2025 statements report 141,666 shares, $3.657 million of rentals, $2.343 million excess cash-basis revenue over expenses, $6.183 million related-party notes receivable, no related-party note payable at year-end, and investments in Apache Railroad and Aztec Minerals. The $3.240 million related-party note payable was the 2024 comparative balance.

Renewable leases have five-to-eight-year development terms, 18-month-to-three-year construction terms, and 25-to-35-year operations terms. Development and construction leases may be cancelled without refund of prior payments.

One wind project entered construction in 2024; one solar project entered development and one solar project began a 30-year operating term in 2025. Most other renewable leases remain in development or construction.
The financial statements use the income-tax basis rather than GAAP and recognize related-party installment gains differently from GAAP, requiring an economic rather than book-value reconciliation.

partially_answered.finding: The audited notes identify related entities, investments, notes, lease stages, and reported cash. They do not provide every project's gross-to-net ownership waterfall, capital obligation, tax, and remittance history required to attribute project NAV to one share.
The total collective surface-acre count is disclosed, but no parcel bridge identifies leased, optioned, inaccessible, environmentally constrained, related-entity-owned, and residual acreage.

The annual report records district water and maintenance costs but does not establish transferable quantity, legal priority, consumptive-use history, delivery capital, buyer demand, net ownership, or realization timing for the modeled water option.

unresolved_material_facts: Project-level ownership and partner waterfalls cannot be reconciled from gross project economics to per-share shareholder cash.
No parcel-level non-overlap map supports the residual acreage component.
No legal and engineering record in the packet supports a transferable, deliverable water quantity or dated monetization path.
Collectability, maturity, and related-party terms for all notes and the realizable value of the railway require further independent testing.
valuation_controls: Use the 239,000 surface acres only as a collective gross control total, never as wholly owned residual acreage.
Separate operating lease cash from projects still in cancellable development or construction stages.
Haircut related-party notes and strategic assets for collectability, minority ownership, tax, and realization timing.
Keep water at a risked option value until legal entitlement, transferability, delivery capital, and net ownership are evidenced.
conclusion: The annual report improves stage classification and entity controls but none of the three follow-up acceptance tests is met. All remain open and the IC should remain evidence-blocked.
